text: No premise-graph bridge definition -- clustered OR clustering-independent -- yields a bridge SET that beats a permutation null. A clustering-INDEPENDENT definition (depth-filtered detector on human Mathlib directory labels, no community detection) does fix the STABILITY problem: its bridge set has Jaccard 0.45-0.68 across three independent file-path partitions (top-level / depth-2 / depth-3 submodules), ~100x more reproducible than the Leiden set's 0.005 vs the same module partition (a-0053). But it FAILS the permutation null exactly as Leiden did: shuffling module labels (size-preserving) reproduces 97% of the observed bridge theorems (null set-recall 0.967), so a theorem's bridge-status is not tied to its TRUE labels. Notably the observed cross-module bridge COUNT is far below random (4773 vs null 23270, z=-301): the genuine cross-domain signal is DEPLETION (premises are domain-local, a-0049), a property of rates/counts, not of any specific theorem set. Conclusion: the operational premise-based bridge cannot be rescued -- 'bridge' reduces to 'theorem with >=2 specialized premises', recovered under random labels. Genuine bridge detection must move to proof-STRUCTURE / conceptual signals (q-0009). SCOPE: tested at theta=2, min cell size 50, on the Mathlib dependency graph; null is label-permutation (size-preserving), not a full config model.
